title: Red-teaming — systematically testing your own system for failure before deployment

artifact_plan.diagram_intent: Cycle diagram of the red-team loop Plan→Attack→Find→Fix→Re-test (regression) looping back to Attack, with the 3-point timeline (before deploy / after updates / ongoing) as surrounding context.
artifact_plan.suggested_profile_signals: title contains 'system'; Core Concepts has 5 sub-concepts (what/why/who/timeline/attack-categories); Implementation 5-step loop; Real-World Patterns warranted; home topic 5.4 deferred red-teaming here; week has a Lab. NOTE: honest beginner word count (~1320) sits in standard band, not deep — builder refused to pad into 5.6/governance scope.
artifact_plan.hitl2_depth_note: Intrinsic signal deep (structural), but downgraded one step to standard at HITL #2: honest beginner length is standard-band and padding would force 5.6/governance scope creep; budget also favors standard (6.4 vs 6.0 min/future). Diagram retained (cycle is visual).

depth_signals: Confirmed standard at HITL #2 (downgraded from structural-deep). Reading ~1200-1600w; diagram kept for the Plan→Attack→Find→Fix→Re-test cycle.
